description: Mending Coherence cost contradiction. canon/02_foundations_amendment_leap_mechanism.md Amendment 3 states Mending Coherence cost is zero (structural consequence of restorative alignment); threadwork_v30 §3.2 lists Mending = -1. Foundations supersedes; §3.2 row stale. Blocks the Edeyja lifetime-Mending capability Foundations argues for. Cross-check §31 on fix.

Mass battle has no Pool Floor; unit deletion (Size->0) is the implicit floor. Pool=min(Size,Cmd)+Cmd floors at 2D (Cmd-1,Size-1), same degenerate math as the faction bare pool (P(>=3|2D)~7%), unlike combat (floor 5) and threadwork (floor 5). Architecturally legitimate but silent in canon and not player-legible. Options: explicit Pool Floor; document+UI surface; Pool Floor=Command. Do not prescribe.

Faction sigma-leverage non-uniform across the faction stat range (armature A6, computed). Bare-stat pool means baseline=stat, so sigma-leverage ~ 1/sqrt(stat): 0.354/pt at stat 2 (+17%) decaying to 0.189/pt at stat 7 (-37%, OUT OF BAND low). Strong factions get leverage-poor stat investment, compounding the punching-up wall. Distinct from the artifact-error findings ED-865/866; this is the underlying design property. Aggregating the pool flattens it.

Mass-battle Command sigma-leverage HIGH at low Command (armature A6, computed): 0.500/pt at Cmd 2 decaying to in-band at Cmd 4-7. Low-Command early-game generals get oversized per-point leverage. Closes attribute_weight_standard GAP #2 (was magnitude-unverified). Non-uniform across range, same 1/sqrt(baseline) mechanism as ED-870.

Continuous-engine continuity correction. REFINES ED-833 (Decision E). Raw Normal(muN, sigma*sqrt N) P(success) runs 2.7-4.4x low vs discrete below 5D and 4-32% low even at validated 5-17D; net-(Ob-0.5) restores agreement to within ~1-3% across pools 1-17 (MC 300k). One-line edit to params/core.md Continuous Engine. No design risk; recommend apply. Closes the prior ER-2 fidelity gap.

MaxWounds cap breaks offense/survival parity at high builds (combat sim, closes derived_stats cross-axis sim-only gap). +1 Agi ~ +1 End at low builds (73% vs 71.5% win) but at high builds +1 Agi=+23pp vs +1 End=+4pp because MW caps at End 4; past the cap Endurance stops buying survival, so min-maxers cap End at 4 and dump into Agi. DEDUPE-CHECK: a prior MW-cap/vitality entry may exist (ED-826/ED-839 territory) — verify before filing as distinct; this is the win-RATE/build-economy framing, sim-magnitude-pending-full-combat-sim.
